engine_response_body: {"error":"blocked","message":"⚠️ 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다.\n\nThis request has been blocked due to sensitive information detected."}
context: W5.1 regression batch — verify PII warning rendering on gemini3 unchanged. Baseline = #1271/#1249/#1228/#1207, all WARNING_NOT_RENDERED (engine block JSON contains '차단' but Gemini BardChatUi FE cannot parse the JSON block body as a protobuf StreamGenerate frame, so no chat bubble renders). #1292 was BLOCKED_INFRA (CDP submit never dispatched StreamGenerate due to cdk-overlay-backdrop email-subscription modal + send-handler gating).
notes: W5.1 REGRESSION CHECK: PASS-FOR-ENGINE / RENDER-GAP-UNCHANGED. Driven via CDP Chrome (port 9222, Chrome 148.0.7778.168, tab 9DF0DDC038FC752E85DD8C644C6C4C84, gemini.google.com/app). RESOLVED #1292 infra blocker by fresh Page.navigate to gemini.google.com/app first (clean load, hasBackdrop=false, hasDialog=false — the email-subscription modal that blocked #1292 was NOT present this session) then a 3-pass overlay-dismiss guard (no overlays found this run) before submit. Login phase: NO '로그인' button (hasLogin=false, hasAvatar=true → logged-in/avatar view, identical to #1271/#1249) — engine intercept is proxy-layer so verdict holds regardless of login. PROMPT INSERT: built the Korean literal from Unicode codepoints (avoid PowerShell/console cp949 mojibake) and inserted via CDP Input.insertText over a UTF-8 websocket. Editor (.ql-editor) held the EXACT 24-char prompt '내 주민등록번호는 901010-1234567' — codepoints [45236,32,51452,48124,46321,47197,48264,54840,45716,32,57,48,49,48,49,48,45,49,50,51,52,53,54,55], BYTE-IDENTICAL to #1271/#1249/#1228/#1207. SUBMIT: located send button by aria-label '메시지 보내기' (center ~1088,365, 32x32, enabled, topIsBtn=true i.e. unobstructed); fired trusted Input.dispatchMouseEvent left-click, then trusted Input.dispatchKeyEvent Enter on the focused editor, then a synthetic JS PointerEvent+MouseEvent+click fallback. StreamGenerate POST fired this run, HTTP 200, requestId 15324.14362, mime application/json, bl=boq_assistant-bard-web-server_20260511.16_p18. ENGINE INTERCEPT FIRED: StreamGenerate response body (Network.getResponseBody, base64Encoded=false, 141 bytes) was the APF block payload {"error":"blocked","message":"⚠️ 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다.\n\nThis request has been blocked due to sensitive information detected."} — contains expected_text '차단' (HAS_CHADAN=true) plus '민감정보' and '보안 정책'. BYTE-IDENTICAL to #1271/#1249/#1228/#1207 engine_response_body — W5.1 changes did NOT alter the gemini3 intercept dispatch or block payload on the StreamGenerate intercept path. HOWEVER warning_rendered=false (unchanged from all 4 baselines): Gemini frontend (BardChatUi expects protobuf StreamGenerate frames) cannot parse the JSON {"error":"blocked"} body → NO chat bubble rendered. DOM after submit: hasBlock=false, hasSensitive=false, hasPolicy=false, convoCount=0, body innerText 74 chars (landing greeting '장희님, 무엇을 도와드릴까요?' + the still-unconsumed prompt + 'Flash'/'제출'); .ql-editor still held the 24-char prompt post-submit (UNCONSUMED). Screenshot results/1314_screenshots/01_final.png (388443 bytes) shows the logged-in landing view with the prompt still in the box and NO warning bubble. CONCLUSION: W5.1 did NOT regress the gemini3 engine block (proxy-layer intercept + payload byte-identical to baseline across 5 runs now: #1207/#1228/#1249/#1271/#1314). The no-render is the SAME pre-existing warning-design gap (BardChatUi cannot parse the JSON block body as a protobuf stream frame), NOT a W5.1 regression. Also confirms #1292 was a transient infra issue (modal overlay), now resolved via fresh-navigate.

baseline_comparison.1271: WARNING_NOT_RENDERED (W4+W6 regression check). #1314 reproduces IDENTICALLY: SAME verdict, SAME BYTE-IDENTICAL 141-byte engine block payload w/ '차단', SAME no-render gap, SAME logged-in/avatar environment, SAME 24-char prompt codepoints, SAME send-button (메시지 보내기 ~1088,365). W5.1 caused NO regression to the gemini3 intercept/block path.
baseline_comparison.1292: BLOCKED_INFRA (CDP submit never dispatched StreamGenerate; cdk-overlay-backdrop email-subscription modal blocked clicks). #1314 RESOLVED this via fresh Page.navigate (clean load, no modal present this session) — submit dispatched StreamGenerate cleanly, so the engine layer was fully exercised this run.
